attacking_style: Early, aggressive attacks from GC groups on extreme gradients (20%+ slopes). Prefers long-range moves with 2km+ to go rather than late punches, targeting breakaway victories on brutal summit finishes.
team_tactics: Operates with limited visible team support in observations. Attacks appear individually initiated rather than team-orchestrated, suggesting freedom to hunt stage wins from breakaways rather than GC protection duties.
terrain_preference: Excels on extreme gradients (20%+ slopes) where pure climbing power decides outcomes. Zoncolan-type savage pitches suit his ability to drop experienced breakaway specialists. Struggles when peloton controls tempo on more moderate climbs.
vulnerability: Attacks neutralized quickly when peloton/favorites maintain controlled tempo. Lacks support to sustain moves when GC teams respond. Limited ability to force selection when stronger teams dictate pace.
key_patterns: Attacks early from GC groups, often before other favorites commit (2 instances)
Drops experienced riders on 20%+ gradients in final 2km (breakaway specialist)
GC attacks consistently neutralized when peloton controls tempo (2 instances)
Targets Grand Tour stage wins from breakaways rather than GC positioning
form_trajectory: unknown: Only 2023 data shows attacks being neutralized, but insufficient recent results to determine if 2021 climbing power on extreme gradients remains
